Pathway_Genes: SbFNSII-1 (CYP93B24): Flavone synthase II isoform 1; cytochrome P450 monooxygenase (CYP93B) with broad substrate specificity converting pinocembrin to chrysin, naringenin to apigenin, and eriodictyol to luteolin; preferred substrate is naringenin (Km 0.28 μM, Vmax 60.93 pkat/mg); RNAi silencing in S. baicalensis hairy roots showed no effect on root-specific flavone (RSF) accumulation, indicating it is not the primary enzyme for 4'-deoxyRSF biosynthesis; expressed at low levels equally across all organs

SbFNSII-2 (CYP93B25): Flavone synthase II isoform 2; cytochrome P450 monooxygenase specific for pinocembrin (4'-deoxyflavanone), converting it to chrysin; cannot use naringenin or eriodictyol as substrates (Km 0.46 μM for pinocembrin, Vmax 9.02 pkat/mg); RNAi silencing in hairy roots reduced baicalin, wogonoside, baicalein, and wogonin by up to 82-95%, and caused accumulation of dihydrobaicalin-O-glucuronide, pinocembrin-O-glucuronide, and dihydrowogonin; highly expressed in roots (8-36 fold higher than aerial organs); induced 4.8-fold by MeJA; heterologous expression in Arabidopsis produced chrysin only when fed pinocembrin; pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana with SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2 produced chrysin

SbCLL-7: Cinnamic acid-specific coenzyme A ligase; converts only cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA with high efficiency (Km 172 μM, Vmax 168 nkat/mg); no activity with 4-coumaric acid or caffeic acid; 27-fold and 9-fold higher Vmax/Km for cinnamic acid compared to SbCLL-1 and SbCLL-5 respectively; RNAi silencing in hairy roots reduced baicalin to 30%, wogonoside to 42%, and baicalein to 14% of control levels; expressed 3-fold higher in roots than aerial organs; not responsive to MeJA; pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana with SbCHS-2 produced pinocembrin, and with SbCHS-2 plus SbFNSII-2 produced chrysin

SbCLL-1: CoA ligase with broad substrate specificity; converts cinnamic acid (Km 5300 μM), 4-coumaric acid (Km 45 μM), and caffeic acid (Km 18 μM); highest expression in stems followed by roots; responsive to MeJA; likely functions in lignin biosynthesis based on substrate preference for 4-coumaric acid and caffeic acid

SbCLL-5: CoA ligase with broad substrate specificity; converts cinnamic acid (Km 3060 μM), 4-coumaric acid (Km 31 μM), and caffeic acid (Km 260 μM); high expression in roots and stems; responsive to MeJA; most closely related to At4CL3
SbCLL-6: CoA ligase-like protein; recombinant protein expressed in E. coli showed no activity toward cinnamic acid, 4-coumaric acid, or caffeic acid
SbCLL-8: CoA ligase-like protein; recombinant protein expressed in E. coli showed no activity toward cinnamic acid, 4-coumaric acid, or caffeic acid

SbCHS-2: Chalcone synthase isoform 2; highly expressed in roots (40887 raw reads in root RNA-seq vs 145 in flowers); induced 16-fold by MeJA; required for pinocembrin production - when co-expressed with SbCLL-7 in N. benthamiana, produced pinocembrin, demonstrating specific activity in using cinnamoyl-CoA that cannot be complemented by endogenous tobacco CHS; 98% identical to SbCHS2a/SbCHS-C and 98% identical to SvCHS from S. viscidula

SbCHS-1: Chalcone synthase isoform 1; expressed specifically in flowers (4038 raw reads in flower RNA-seq vs 45 in roots); induced 6-fold by MeJA; 83% identity with SbCHS-2; likely involved in standard 4'-hydroxyflavone biosynthesis in aerial tissues

SbCHI: Chalcone isomerase; single isoform identified in S. baicalensis roots; used in pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana but shown to be non-essential as endogenous tobacco CHI could substitute; pinocembrin formation from pinocembrin chalcone occurs either spontaneously or through this CHI
Title: A specialized flavone biosynthetic pathway has evolved in the medicinal plant, Scutellaria baicalensis
